name: 03_rename_and_observers
description: Candidate joins as 'SP', renames to nickname 'Sam' mid-call. Two silent camera-off observers join. Panel of two interviewers. Tests rename handling (identity keyed on participant ID) and observer elimination.

data.text: Welcome! A couple of colleagues are shadowing today, they'll stay muted. Can you share a bit about your background?

Of course. I'm Sam, I've spent the last six years in data platform teams. In my current role I led the migration of our warehouse to a lakehouse architecture, and I built the ingestion framework the whole company uses now.
Can you walk me through how you handled schema evolution in that framework?

We versioned every schema in a registry, and I implemented compatibility checks in CI so producers could not deploy breaking changes. Consumers pinned major versions and we ran dual writes during migrations.
What would you do differently if you rebuilt it today?
Honestly, I would push more logic into the table format itself. Iceberg has matured a lot, and my team spent effort maintaining code that the format now gives you for free.
